candidate_summary: Aqueduct-gradient inspired monotonically decreasing per-token compute budget with calibrated slope and mid-decode gradient correction

evaluations.judge_reason: Pure source-domain Wikipedia article on Roman aqueducts; no LLM/compute-budget content, but high source-side keyword overlap.
Source-domain article about a specific Roman aqueduct; no LLM mechanism overlap, but matches source-side terms.
Source-domain blog about chorobates and aqueduct gradient; no LLM analogy attempted.
BudgetThinker uses control tokens reminding model of remaining budget — related but uses learned RL controller, not an explicit monotonic calibrated slope with mid-decode chorobates-style correction.
SelfBudgeter allocates budget by problem complexity — adaptive, not monotonic-decreasing per-token with calibrated slope.
DiffAdapt picks discrete easy/normal/hard strategies by difficulty — categorical, not a calibrated continuous monotonic gradient.
Broad survey of adaptive test-time compute; may discuss many approaches but no specific monotonic-decreasing slope mechanism with mid-decode correction.
Classifier-based mid-generation adaptation via constrained policy optimization — related theme but learned policy not calibrated slope; URL date 2026/4 is suspicious.

notes: Three source-domain results clear keyword threshold but score very low on functional equivalence and semantic cosine — none reaches hit=true on the combined criterion.
